text:
UAR—Africa: Cairo is continuing its clandestine financial support of African nationalists. more than $140,000 has been turned over to Ugandan, Kenyan, and Northern and Southern Rhodesian nationalist leaders during the past three months.

the UAR also informed its diplomatic missions that while it would support a proposed Ghanaian UN resolution recommending that member nations sever relations with South Africa, it would try to keep open its legation in Johannesburg as "the only official means of contact" between South African nationalists and the outside world.

sought Brazilian support for an Indonesian draft resolution to be submitted in the UN General Assembly on the Cuban complaint against the United States. This draft resolution has not yet been formally presented. Mexico is willing to support the draft and that Brazilian backing would influence other Latin American governments to do likewise. Brazil's customary position has been not to refer hemispheric problems to the UN until appeals to the OAS had been exhausted. In reply Castro claimed that the OAS is incapable of defending small countries when their rights "are disregarded by the largest power on the continent

text:
to do likewise. Brazil's customary position has been not to refer hemispheric problems to the UN until appeals to the OAS had been exhausted. In reply Castro claimed that the OAS is incapable of defending small countries when their rights "are disregarded by the largest power on the continent".

The Cuban delegation in the UN passed up an opportunity on 5 April to press for early consideration of its charges against the US before the General Assembly's main political committee, probably preferring to wait until it had lined up support among a larger number of UN members for a resolution acceptable to Cuba.

Cuba: The pace of anti-Castro activity both inside Cuba and among Cuban exiles is mounting.

new outbreaks of guerrilla activity in Oriente Province and in the border area of Las Villas and Matanzas provinces since mid-March. considerable information on insurgent plans in various provinces. Clandestine anti-Castro radio stations have referred to plans for coordinated acts of sabotage in Havana
